educations.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science

description: Own the end-to-end ML lifecycle for an augmented-reality virtual try-on feature — dataset curation and labeling, training, evaluation, deployment, and monitoring — taking models from prototype to production on a live camera feed.
Built PyTorch training and fine-tuning pipelines with augmentation, loss tuning, and hyperparameter search, lifting model accuracy from 79% to 88% (0.89 mIoU) on a 5K-image validation set.
Cut serving latency 45% (110ms to 60ms) and model size 65% through INT8 quantization, pruning, and ONNX/TensorRT export, sustaining 25–30 FPS real-time inference on mid-range mobile devices.
Built MLOps tooling — automated labeling, training, and evaluation with MLflow experiment tracking — cutting the retrain-and-evaluate cycle from 2 days to 6 hours.
Deployed containerized models on Kubernetes with data-drift monitoring, and validated releases through A/B testing, driving a 14% lift in product-page engagement in a pilot rollout.
Delivered a loan approval prediction model reaching 0.86 ROC-AUC, benchmarking Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost across 5-fold cross-validation.
Lifted default-class recall from 68% to 81% by engineering features from income, credit history, and employment attributes and countering class imbalance with SMOTE resampling.
Tuned decision thresholds to reduce projected default risk 12% at a comparable approval rate, and used feature-importance analysis to explain predictions to reviewers.
